title: Prediction Markets: The Potential Multi‑Trillion Dollar Asset Class Hiding In Plain Sight
gist: ARK argues that prediction markets' current sports-betting traction is mostly regulatory arbitrage masking a far bigger opportunity: becoming unbundled financial infrastructure that could scale to $1-5 trillion (up to $22.6 trillion bull case) within 3-5 years by competing with the global OTC derivatives market.

insights.claim: US adults' trust in information from national news organizations is near all-time lows, according to Pew Research Center data cited by ARK.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Declining trust in legacy information gatekeepers is the demand-side tailwind for capital-backed truth discovery mechanisms like prediction markets.
insights.locator: Data from the Pew Research Center suggests

insights.claim: Only 22% of Americans trust the federal government to do the right thing most or all of the time, with confidence in government near historic lows for more than 15 years.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Persistent, structural distrust in official institutions (not a one-off event) supports a durable rather than cyclical growth case for crowd-priced information markets.
insights.locator: Confidence in government has been near historic lows

insights.claim: ARK's core thesis is that prediction markets function as a truth-discovery mechanism backed by real capital rather than opinion, and their real potential is as future financial infrastructure worth up to roughly $5 trillion medium-term.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Frames prediction markets as an information-integrity asset class, not just a betting product, which is the lens ARK uses to justify a multi-trillion-dollar TAM.
insights.quote: we believe prediction markets’ real potential will come from their instantiation as the future of financial infrastructure, presenting a potential medium-term opportunity up to ~$5 trillion
insights.locator: we believe prediction markets’ real potential

themes.theme: Historical Precedent for Disguised Financial Innovation

insights.claim: Prediction markets are centuries old: 16th-century Italians traded on who would succeed the next pope, and 18th-century England's Jonathan's Coffee House traded on parliamentary scandals and prime-minister changes before evolving into the London Stock Exchange.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Establishes that today's crypto-native prediction markets (e.g., Polymarket) sit in a long lineage of informal betting markets maturing into formal financial exchanges.
insights.locator: The concept of prediction markets is centuries old

insights.claim: In the early 1980s Ray Dalio helped McDonald's hedge volatile chicken feed costs using corn and soybean futures, a financial-engineering breakthrough disguised as a menu innovation that gave McDonald's confidence to launch Chicken McNuggets nationally; ARK uses this as an analogy for how prediction markets' true significance as financial infrastructure may be underappreciated today.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Suggests investors should look past prediction markets' consumer-facing sports/politics use case to the underlying risk-management infrastructure being built, since the largest value creation may be invisible at first.
insights.locator: In the early 1980s, volatile chicken feed prices

themes.theme: Sports Betting Is Regulatory Arbitrage, Not the Endgame

insights.claim: ARK explicitly argues prediction markets will not disrupt sports betting; instead they will become a foundational layer of financial services impacting markets well beyond sports.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Counters the narrative that Kalshi/Polymarket are primarily sportsbook disruptors — investors modeling these platforms as OSB competitors are using the wrong comp set.
insights.quote: we argue that prediction markets will not disrupt sports betting and that they could become a foundational layer of financial services, impacting markets well beyond sports
insights.locator: we argue that prediction markets will not disrupt

insights.claim: Kalshi's weekly notional sports-contract volume catapulted 21x since the start of the September 2025 NFL season, making sports the dominant driver of platform volume.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Shows the scale of Kalshi's near-term growth engine, which ARK argues is temporary and regulatory rather than structural.
insights.locator: sports contracts have been the standout volume driver

insights.claim: ARK's research concludes Kalshi's sports volume is largely regulatory arbitrage: because CFTC oversight is federal, Kalshi's sports event contracts can bypass state-by-state gambling approval in the 30-32 states (~40% of the US population) that lack legal online sports betting.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: If courts eventually classify these contracts as gambling subject to state law, Kalshi's current sports volume base could compress sharply — a key regulatory risk to watch.
insights.quote: Kalshi’s current sports event contract volume is a function largely of regulatory arbitrage in states which have not legalized online sports betting
insights.locator: Our research suggests that sports volumes on Kalshi

insights.claim: According to YipitData, roughly 60% of Kalshi's 2025 deposit dollars came from states without legal sports betting, rising even higher during March Madness and the NFL season.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Concrete evidence that Kalshi's user base skews toward markets where legal alternatives don't exist, supporting the regulatory-arbitrage thesis over genuine product superiority.
insights.locator: According to YipitData, the percentage of Kalshi's deposit dollars

insights.claim: Even in the ~40% of states where DraftKings operates legally, Kalshi's deposit volume is only a fraction of DraftKings', indicating Kalshi is not out-competing incumbent sportsbooks where a level playing field exists.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Undercuts any bull case that treats Kalshi as a superior sports-betting product; its edge is jurisdictional, not competitive.
insights.locator: Furthermore, based on the ~40% from legal OSB states

insights.claim: Sports fell from 90% of total Kalshi volume at the end of 2025 to roughly 75% in March 2026, even during March Madness, implying non-sports (financial/political) contracts are growing faster than sports on the platform.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Early evidence the platform's mix is already shifting toward the financial-infrastructure use case ARK is betting on, ahead of any regulatory resolution.
insights.locator: Sports as a percent of total Kalshi volume has dropped

themes.theme: Regulatory Battles Are Tilting Toward Kalshi

insights.claim: A Third Circuit decision in New Jersey ruled that Kalshi's sports event contracts are swaps falling under the CFTC's exclusive jurisdiction, limiting New Jersey's ability to regulate them as sports betting.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: A key legal precedent favoring federal (CFTC) over state gambling jurisdiction reduces near-term existential regulatory risk to Kalshi's sports business.
insights.locator: a recent Third Circuit decision in New Jersey

insights.claim: A federal judge granted the CFTC's request for a temporary restraining order blocking Arizona from pursuing criminal charges against Kalshi.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Federal regulators are actively shielding Kalshi from state enforcement, a bullish near-term signal even as the broader legal landscape remains unsettled.
insights.locator: In Arizona, a federal judge granted a CFTC request

insights.claim: Multiple unresolved state-level court cases (Arizona, Connecticut, Illinois, Nevada, Massachusetts, and others) are contesting whether sports event contracts fall under CFTC or state gambling jurisdiction, and ARK cautions these rulings could still change.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: The regulatory outcome across these state cases is the single biggest swing factor for Kalshi's sports-contract volume and should be tracked case-by-case.
insights.locator: multiple state-level cases are moving through US courts

insights.claim: Traditional derivatives bundle multiple risk factors (the Greeks, underlying price, and myriad catalysts) into one instrument, forcing an investor with a single-catalyst thesis to absorb unrelated volatility; prediction markets instead unbundle risk into direct exposure on one discrete binary outcome.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Positions prediction markets as a distinct product category from options/derivatives — a purer, more isolated way to express a single-catalyst view, which is the structural reason ARK expects institutional adoption over time.
insights.locator: the derivative contract is a composite of the Greeks

insights.claim: A Tesla (TSLA) option's price is pulled by interest rates, delivery milestones, EV competitor pricing, Elon Musk's social media activity, and ventures like Robotaxi or Optimus all at once, whereas a prediction market contract such as 'Will Tesla Optimus robots be released by Dec 31, 2026?' isolates just that one catalyst.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Illustrates concretely how a retail investor could take a pure view on one Tesla catalyst (e.g., Optimus timeline) without also being exposed to unrelated stock volatility.
insights.locator: Consider a Tesla (TSLA) option

insights.claim: ARK views prediction markets as new financial primitives whose most transformative future applications likely do not exist yet.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Signals ARK expects the biggest value creation to come from as-yet-unbuilt use cases, meaning today's sports/politics contracts undersell the category's long-run TAM.
insights.locator: As new financial primitives, the most transformational applications

insights.claim: Signal quality in prediction markets scales with liquidity: high liquidity aggregates diverse participant views into accurate pricing, while low liquidity lets noise from uninformed or marginal traders dominate.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Gives investors a concrete filter for which prediction-market prices are trustworthy signal versus noise: check open interest/volume before treating a contract's implied probability as informative.
insights.locator: High liquidity produces strong signal

insights.claim: Kalshi and Polymarket concentrate the majority of prediction-market liquidity but via structurally different models: Kalshi relies on professional market makers and institutional partners under strict CFTC compliance (with Robinhood and Coinbase already partnered), while Polymarket's liquidity is driven organically by community/retail flow through blockchain smart contracts and passive central limit order books.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: The two dominant platforms are not interchangeable bets — Kalshi's model depends on institutional/MM partnerships and regulatory compliance, Polymarket's depends on retail network effects and blockchain infrastructure.
insights.locator: The majority of prediction market liquidity is concentrated

insights.claim: ARK predicts professional market makers will be essential to scaling prediction markets, especially for complex or multi-leg contracts.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Points to market-making infrastructure (and firms that provide it, e.g., via Kalshi partnerships) as a chokepoint/beneficiary as the asset class scales toward complex financial contracts.
insights.locator: Professional market makers (MMs) will be essential

insights.claim: Relative liquidity across simultaneous event contracts — e.g., a week with both a company earnings report and an FOMC rate decision — can itself signal which catalyst the market believes will move a stock more.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Suggests a novel data source: watching where liquidity concentrates across competing event contracts could reveal market-implied catalyst importance before price action confirms it.
insights.locator: During a week when a company is scheduled to report earnings

themes.theme: Market Sizing: From Sports Novelty to Multi-Trillion TAM

insights.claim: In 2025, sports-related prediction market volume grew ~7x and non-sports volume grew ~2.5x; extrapolating Q1 2026 run-rates for the full year, ARK projects sports contracts growing ~5x and non-sports contracts growing ~6x in 2026, with non-sports overtaking sports growth.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: The crossover where non-sports growth outpaces sports growth is the empirical signal ARK points to for product-market fit in financial/political use cases, not just sports.
insights.locator: In 2025, prediction markets entered the mainstream

insights.claim: ARK sizes prediction markets' TAM by benchmarking against global OTC derivatives volume, modeling the addressable share (25%-100%) against an adoption rate (0.5%-2.5%), producing a sensitivity range from about $1.1 trillion to $22.6 trillion.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Gives investors the explicit model inputs (addressable share x adoption rate) to stress-test ARK's TAM claim themselves, rather than taking a single headline number on faith.
insights.locator: To estimate the potential market size, our benchmark

insights.claim: ARK anchors its medium-term (3-5 year) prediction-market opportunity estimate conservatively at $1-5 trillion, near the low end of its own sensitivity range, citing the pace of institutional adoption as the biggest uncertainty.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: ARK is deliberately hedging toward the conservative end of its own model — a re-rating toward the $10-20 trillion range would require a visible inflection in institutional participation, a concrete catalyst to monitor.
insights.quote: We therefore size the potential medium-term opportunity at approximately $1 trillion to $5 trillion, anchoring to the lower end of the sensitivity range
insights.locator: We therefore size the potential medium-term opportunity

insights.claim: Robinhood (HOOD) and Coinbase (COIN) already have partnerships with Kalshi, which ARK reads as validation of Kalshi's regulatory-compliant, market-maker-centric model for scaling liquidity under current US regulation.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Both COIN and HOOD have direct exposure to prediction-market growth today via Kalshi partnerships, making them the most direct public-equity proxies for ARK's thesis even before dedicated prediction-market pure-plays exist.
insights.locator: Robinhood and Coinbase already have partnerships with Kalshi
